strengths: 15+ years of polyglot full-stack experience across Node.js/TypeScript, multiple frameworks, embedded systems, and cloud infrastructure — well above the 6-year floor
Current role at re:cinq includes direct AI-agent infrastructure work (MCP code generators, agentic workflow frameworks), matching the JD's AI agent development requirement with concrete evidence
Sustained testing culture: TDD practitioner at RIDE Capital, authored the D-language 'Trial' test runner and 'Fluent-Asserts' library, taught multi-module TDD/Selenium courses
concerns: Nest.js is entirely absent from the résumé despite being the primary backend framework in the required stack — represents a concrete ramp-up cost on day one
React surfaces only once (Optilyz, no depth described); frontend history is Ember.js-dominant, signaling the candidate may need to shift their primary UI mental model
Kotlin is not mentioned at all; Java appears briefly in a robotics context at Altom (2016–2018) and is not positioned as an active competency
response.justification: The candidate has a credible, senior-level Node.js/TypeScript track record across Optilyz, RIDE Capital, and re:cinq, and the current role's AI-agent and MCP tooling work is a direct hit on a differentiating JD requirement. Cloud (AWS CDK, Kubernetes, Docker) and MongoDB database experience are well-evidenced. The blocking gap is Nest.js — the primary framework listed in the stack — which does not appear anywhere in the résumé; a developer moving from raw Express/Node patterns to Nest.js typically needs several weeks to internalize its DI and module conventions. React depth is also thinner than expected for a mid-senior fullstack role where React is listed as primary. The testing instincts are clearly strong but framework-specific names (Vitest, Jest, Playwright) are absent. Score reflects a highly experienced engineer with a meaningful stack mismatch on the two most-used daily tools.
